description_long: This object is a large solar carport structure designed to provide both shade and renewable energy generation in urban environments. The main feature is a wide, flat canopy composed of multiple photovoltaic solar panels arranged in a grid, supported by a robust metal framework with angled struts. The structure is elevated above a paved platform, allowing vehicles or pedestrians to pass or park beneath it. The supporting frame appears to be made of steel or aluminum, with visible truss elements for stability. The surface beneath the panels is a flat, concrete-like base, possibly for parking or pedestrian use. The overall design is utilitarian and modern, optimized for maximum solar exposure and structural integrity.
description: A large, elevated solar carport with a grid of photovoltaic panels supported by a metal frame over a paved base. Designed for urban energy generation and vehicle shelter.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the solar panel canopy and the supporting frame, with the paved base visible below.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the angled support struts and the depth of the structure, with the grid of solar panels extending across the top.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the back of the solar panel array and the supporting framework.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the angled supports and the length of the solar panel canopy.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 10-15 degrees, orientation appears fixed, azimuth not clearly specified
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with angled steel or aluminum posts and truss supports
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of approximately 8-10 large panels arranged side by side
